hook.text: We can't dig to the core. So how do we know what's down there? Earthquakes told us, through their waves.

buildup.text: Seismic waves travel through Earth after earthquakes. P-waves compress rock; S-waves shake it side to side.

discovery.text: P-waves pass through liquids, but S-waves don't. When S-waves vanish, we know they hit liquid outer core.

twist.text: By mapping where waves speed up, slow down, or vanish, we built a complete picture of Earth's interior.

climax.text: Seismic waves are geology's X-ray. Every earthquake reveals a little more about the hidden planet below.

punchline.text: Earthquakes destroy, but they also reveal.

quiz.question: How do we know Earth's outer core is liquid?
options: We can see it from space
